---
title: "The legacy of Dijkstra"
author:
  - name: Ralph Straumann
    url: https://ralphstraumann.ch
date: "2026-05-31 11:56"
image: "dimitri-karastelev-4IqOnX2sdyM-unsplash.jpg"
description: "Edsger Dijkstra's landmark shortest-path algorithm has shaped how we navigate the world, from early GIS to today's wayfinding apps. A new Veritasium video traces that legacy and reveals how techniques like Nested Dissection and Contraction Hierarchies deliver routing results in microseconds."
---

If you've studied [routing algorithms][routing], you've come across [Edsger 
Dijkstra's algorithm][algo], a fundamental method for finding the shortest paths 
between nodes in a graph or network. This on its surface very simple algorithm 
has had a profound impact and has been widely used in various applications, from 
navigation aids to GIS.

In their most recent video, *"How does Google Maps actually work?"*, popular 
science YouTube channel [Veritasium][veritasium] explores the origin of 
Dijkstra's algorithm, how it laid the groundwork for modern routing techniques 
used in wayfinding apps and how techniques such as Nested Dissection and 
Customisable Contraction Hierarchies enable microsecond response times for route 
queries in large road networks:

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kS-CGkiPetQ >}}

[routing]: https://spatialists.ch/posts/2025/10/02-routing-algorithms-visualized/
[algo]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dijkstra%27s_algorithm
[veritasium]: https://www.youtube.com/@veritasium/featured
